text.title: LLM Cost Reduction Strategies Core Rules

rule.statement: Response cache key MUST be SHA-256 of (prompt + model + temperature + system_prompt).
rule.rationale: Hash collisions on shorter keys cause cross-tenant leakage.

rule.statement: Cache TTL MUST be bounded (e.g. 24h) — never permanent.
rule.rationale: Stale answers cause regressions when prompt-template or model changes.

rule.statement: Prompt compression MUST start with whitespace + boilerplate removal before any LLM-based summarisation.
rule.rationale: Mechanical compression is free; LLM-based compression costs tokens.

rule.statement: Async batching MUST be applied only to calls with no user waiting.
Cheap-model routing MUST require a confidence gate (LLM-as-judge or eval-harness pass) before falling back to the expensive model.
rule.rationale: Unconditional cheap-model use degrades quality silently.

rule.statement: Cost-savings report MUST attribute $ saved per lever (cache hit rate × call cost; routed-to-cheap ratio × delta; etc.).
rule.rationale: Aggregate savings hide which lever is actually working.
